configEditor.noConfigLoaded: No config file loaded. Use the template selector above to load a store template or one of your saved templates.

doctor.title: System Health
doctor.subtitle: Prerequisites and dependency checks
doctor.allChecksPassed: All checks passed — system is ready

images.title: Images
images.subtitle: Available container images for agents
images.emptyDescription: Images are defined in your templates and configuration

runtimes.subtitle: Available agent runtimes and their default images

store.title: Agents Store
store.heroTitle: Deploy AI Agent Teams in One Click
store.searchPlaceholder: Search templates by name, category, or feature...
typewriterPhrases: Find a code review team template...
store.recommendedDescription: High-signal templates to help you launch faster.
store.noTemplatesInCategory: No templates available in this category.
store.allTemplatesOpenSource: All templates are open-source and maintained by the Shadow Cloud team.
store.customTemplatesNote: Custom templates can be added via
store.communityUnavailable: Community server unavailable. Showing local templates.

storeDetail.templateNotFoundDesc: The template does not exist or is not available.
storeDetail.deployToSeeConfig: Deploy this template to see the full agent configuration.
storeDetail.fullTemplateConfig: Full template configuration. This will be used as the base config for deployment.

deploy.confirmDeployKubernetes: Review the deployment you are about to start.
deploy.setNamespaceEnv: Confirm the deployment name and fill the required variables.
deploy.kubernetesNamespaceDesc: Use this name to separate this deployment. The default usually works well.
deploy.envVarsAllRequired: All must be provided before deployment.
deploy.pluginFieldDescription: Connects this service. Saved secrets can be reused.
deploy.pluginFieldGroupDescription: {{plugin}} connection credentials. Saved secrets are reused automatically.
deploy.autoDetectedVarsDescription: Existing configuration is reused, so these do not need to be filled here.
deploy.secretPersistenceDescription: New values can be saved to "{{group}}" and reused automatically next time.
deploy.rememberSecretsDescription: Save filled values to the "{{group}}" reusable group.
deploy.bindSecretsToNamespaceDescription: Also save values as namespace overrides after deploy.
deploy.secretSaveFailed: Deployment succeeded, but some secrets could not be saved.
deploy.optionalKeyValue: Optional key-value pairs beyond the required ones.
deploy.deployingToCluster: Deploying your agent team to the cluster...
deploy.deploySuccessDesc: Your agent team has been deployed successfully.
deploy.deployFailDesc: Deployment encountered an error. Check the logs below.
deploy.reviewConfig: Review your configuration and start the deployment.
deploy.whatHappensNextDesc: Shadow Cloud will initialize the template, generate Kubernetes manifests, and deploy them to your cluster. You can monitor the progress in real time below.
deploy.shadowConnectionDescription: Server URL and API token for provisioning Shadow resources (servers, channels, buddies).
deploy.agentRunningInNamespace: Your agent team is now running in the {{namespace}} namespace.
deploy.openNamespaceDescription: Inspect agents, logs, environment values, and cost details.
deploy.shadowServerReadyDescription: The Shadow server is ready. Open the new server to start using it.
deploy.namespaceReadyDescription: Deployment is complete. Open the {{namespace}} namespace to inspect runtime status.
deploy.providersDescription: Configure at least one LLM provider so your agents can connect to model APIs.
deploy.modelProviderDescription: Choose how this template connects to models before deployment.
deploy.modelProviderOfficialDescription: Use the official OpenAI-compatible proxy, with usage billed from your Shrimp Coin wallet.
deploy.modelProviderOfficialPricingTokens: Billing: input {{input}} tokens / Shrimp Coin, output {{output}} tokens / Shrimp Coin.
deploy.modelProviderOfficialPricingDeepSeek: Billing: cached input {{cacheHit}} Shrimp Coins / 1M tokens, regular input {{cacheMiss}} Shrimp Coins / 1M tokens, output {{output}} Shrimp Coins / 1M tokens. Exchange: 1 CNY = {{shrimpPerCny}} Shrimp Coins.
deploy.modelProviderExchangeRate: Exchange rate: 1 CNY = {{shrimpPerCny}} Shrimp Coins. Small token usage is accumulated precisely before it becomes a whole-coin wallet charge.
deploy.modelProviderOfficialWalletHint: The official provider is ready to use after deployment. It never exposes the upstream provider key to your server.
deploy.modelProviderCustomDescription: Use your own encrypted provider profile and bill usage directly through that provider.
deploy.modelProviderCustomSelfPaidNote: Usage is billed through your own provider account.
deploy.modelProviderCustomSecureNote: Provider keys are stored securely and are not shown in this deployment form.
deploy.customProviderRequired: Please configure an enabled model provider before using your own provider.
deploy.providerProfilesReadyDesc: A usable model configuration is available.
deploy.providerProfilesMissingDesc: Add a model configuration now, or continue filling in variables.
deploy.providersUseExistingDescription: Your existing provider settings will be used automatically.
deploy.cancelRequested: Cancellation requested. Waiting for the deployment to stop.
deploy.insufficientBalanceDesc: You don't have enough Shrimp Coins to deploy this app. Top up to continue.
deploy.rechargeUnavailable: Recharge is only available inside the Shadow app.

clusters.title: Cluster Management
clusters.description: Manage agent deployments across all Kubernetes namespaces.
clusters.noDeploymentsYet: No agent deployments yet. Deploy your first agent team from the store.
clusters.destroyWarning: This will destroy all deployments in namespace "{{namespace}}". This action cannot be undone.

deployments.title: Deployments
deployments.namespaceDescription: Namespace-level operations for all agents, logs, environment values, and costs.
deployments.agentSelectorDescription: Pick an agent to inspect pods and logs without leaving this namespace.
deployments.noAgentSelected: Choose an agent to inspect this namespace.
deployments.selectAgentForLogs: Select an agent to load historical and live logs.
deployments.logsHistoryDescription: Browse recent log history for {{agent}} with pagination.
deployments.liveLogsDescription: Connect to the selected agent to stream new log lines in real time.
deployments.scopedEnvDescription: These values are stored for this namespace only and override any global defaults.
deployments.fallbackEnvDescription: These values come from broader scopes and are applied when no namespace override exists.
deployments.noTasksInNamespace: This namespace has no deploy tasks yet.
deployments.costUnavailableDescription: Usage data could not be collected from the running agents yet.
deployments.costNoRunningPod: No running pod is available for usage collection.
deployments.costUsageUnavailableMessage: OpenClaw has not returned token usage yet. Refresh after the agent handles a message.
deployments.costTelemetryMissing: No usage snapshot has been reported yet. Refresh after this Buddy handles a new message.
deployments.liveLogsUnavailableTransport: The Kubernetes log stream is temporarily unavailable. Retry later or use recent logs.
deployments.noDeploymentsInNamespace: No deployments found in this namespace
deployments.noDeploymentsInNamespaceDescription: Namespace "{{namespace}}" does not currently have any active agents.
deployments.noTaskToRedeploy: No deploy task found for this namespace
deployments.rollbackWarning: This will roll back all deployments in namespace "{{namespace}}" to the previous state.
deployments.configDescription: Edit the cloud configuration JSON. Changes take effect after redeployment.

deploymentDetail.podsEmptyDescription: The deployment may be scaling up.
deploymentDetail.logsDescription: Real-time log stream from all pods in this deployment.

templates.title: My Templates
templates.description: Fork templates from the Agent Store, customize them, and deploy your own versions.
templates.createTemplateDescription: Create a blank template and start editing it in your workspace.
templates.chooseStoreTemplate: Choose a store template to fork. You'll get an editable copy.
templates.noCustomTemplateHint: Create from scratch or fork from the store to get started.
templates.cloneGitRepository: Clone a git repository and import the template config.
templates.deleteConfirm: Delete template "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
templates.deleteApprovedConfirm: Template "{{name}}" is published in the community store. Deleting it will immediately remove it from the store and all users will lose access. This cannot be undone.
templates.deletePendingConfirm: Template "{{name}}" is currently under review. Deleting it will cancel the review submission. This action cannot be undone.
templates.searchSavedPlaceholder: Search saved templates by name, source, or namespace...
templates.recentlyUpdatedDescription: Jump back into what you edited most recently and keep shipping.
templates.emptyFiltered: No saved templates match the current filters.
templates.shareCopied: Template JSON copied to clipboard — share with others!

templateDetail.editAndSaveToCreate: Edit and save the template to create version history.
templateDetail.configPreviewDescription: Review the shared JSON source that powers this custom template.
templateDetail.forkedDescription: Forked from {{name}} and ready for your custom edits and redeploys.
templateDetail.customDescription: A custom template stored in your workspace, ready to edit, version, and deploy.
templateDetail.noAgents: No agent definitions found in this template.
templateDetail.publishSuccess: Template submitted for review. The admin will review it shortly.
templateDetail.publishFailed: Failed to submit. Please try again.
templateDetail.publishTooltip: Submit this template for community review
templateDetail.publishConfirmDescription: Your template will be submitted for admin review. It won't be visible publicly until approved.
templateDetail.resubmitConfirmDescription: You are resubmitting this template after the previous rejection. Please ensure you've addressed the feedback.
templateDetail.rejectionBannerDesc: Please review the admin's feedback below, make changes, and resubmit.
templateDetail.pendingBannerDesc: Your template is currently being reviewed. You cannot edit it until the review is complete.
templateDetail.approvedBannerDesc: Your template is live in the community store.
templateDetail.editorLockedPending: Editing is disabled while your template is under review.
templateDetail.editorLockedApproved: This template is published and cannot be edited.

secrets.title: Secrets and Environment Variables
secrets.description: Keep deployment credentials and environment variables together in a secure workspace.
secrets.allSecretsEncrypted: All secrets are encrypted at rest using platform-managed keys.
secrets.envVarToUseCustom: Set this environment variable to use a custom passphrase.
secrets.deleteGroupDescription: This will permanently delete the "{{group}}" group and all its environment variables. This cannot be undone.
secrets.providerSecretsDesc: API keys and credentials for LLM providers, saved with the same protection.
secrets.noSecretsInGroup: No secrets in this group yet
secrets.environmentValuesDescription: Store deployment keys and variables in one place. Use secret mode for sensitive values.
secrets.globalScopedEnvVars: Global and scoped environment variables injected into deployments.
secrets.addEnvironmentValueDescription: A new value will be added to the "{{group}}" group.
secrets.editEnvironmentValueDescription: Update the value stored in the "{{group}}" group.
secrets.noEnvVarsInGroup: No variables in this group yet
secrets.emptyDescription: Add values to this group to start managing deployment secrets and environment variables, which are stored securely and organized by group.
secrets.leaveEmptyKeep: Leave empty to keep current value

providers.title: Model providers
providers.connectProvidersDescription: Choose a provider, then paste its API key. We will validate and enable it automatically.
providers.apiKeyProvidersDescription: Choose a provider first, paste your API key, and save to validate before using it.
providers.customProviderDescription: Supports OpenAI-compatible APIs, including private or gateway-based URLs.
providers.noLocalProvidersDescription: Add a custom compatible endpoint using localhost or private network access.
providers.profileDialogDescription: Set the provider key, endpoint, and model mapping here. Changes apply to future deployments.
providers.connectionDescription: These settings are used during tests and deployments.
providers.encryptionDescription: They are decrypted only during tests and deployments.
providers.officialProviderDescription: Use Shadow's built-in OpenAI-compatible gateway. Usage is charged from your Shrimp Coin wallet.
providers.officialPricingTokens: Official billing: input {{input}} tokens / Shrimp Coin, output {{output}} tokens / Shrimp Coin.
providers.officialPricingDeepSeek: Official billing: cached input {{cacheHit}} Shrimp Coins / 1M tokens, regular input {{cacheMiss}} Shrimp Coins / 1M tokens, output {{output}} Shrimp Coins / 1M tokens. Exchange: 1 CNY = {{shrimpPerCny}} Shrimp Coins.
providers.noProfilesDescription: Add one compatible provider such as OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, or Qwen to get started.
providers.basicDescription: Choose a provider and enter its API key.
providers.profileNamePlaceholder: Give this connection a clear name, such as OpenAI.
providers.apiKeyEditPlaceholder: Already saved. Enter a new key only if you want to replace it
providers.apiKeyHelp: For security, this field is masked after saving. It is replaced only when you type a new key.
providers.baseUrlHelp: Leave blank to use the default endpoint. You can also use your own gateway URL.
providers.modelsDescription: Keep at least one model ID. These are used by your deployments.
providers.modelTagHint: Use tags to organize models by scenario. Matching by model ID still works.
providers.modelRequired: Please add at least one model.
providers.routingDescription: Pick models for simple, standard, complex, and reasoning work.
providers.routingManifestDescription: {{count}} › Pick which model handles each type of request

routingModeDescriptions.taskSpecific: Configure dedicated models for coding, browsing, analysis, and other task types. Phase 1 reuses the complexity route model pool.
routingModeDescriptions.custom: Later this can add request rules by header, template, or Agent.

providers.routeByComplexityDescription: Analyzes the complexity of each request on the fly and routes it to the matching tier.
providers.noFallbacksDescription: Add fallback models to guarantee a response if the provider fails.
providers.keepDefaults: If you prefer, keep the default settings.
providers.routingLoadFailedDescription: Could not read model routing and budget settings. Please retry.

routeDescriptions.simple: Fast, cheap traffic such as greetings and status checks.
routeDescriptions.complex: Higher quality tasks with more context or nuance.

providers.selectorPlaceholder: Tag selector, e.g. fast or reasoning
providers.limitsDescription: Phase 1 stores the contract used by future proxy enforcement and billing dashboards.
providers.limitsManifestDescription: {{count}} › Get notified or block requests when token or cost thresholds are exceeded
providers.createRuleDescription: You'll receive an email alert when usage exceeds the threshold.
providers.connectProviderForHardLimits: Connect a provider to set hard limits
providers.connectProviderForHardLimitsDescription: Hard limits automatically block proxy requests when usage exceeds a threshold. Email alerts work without a connected provider; only hard limits require one.
providers.noRulesYetDescription: Set up alerts for usage spikes, or hard limits to block requests over budget.
providers.fallbackPolicyDescription: Fallback on these upstream status codes before surfacing an error to the agent.
providers.modelPricesDescription: Compare saved model costs used by routing.
providers.noModelPrices: Refresh or add models to see pricing here.
providers.deleteDescription: This deletes "{{name}}" and its encrypted keys. This cannot be undone.

monitoring.title: Dashboard
monitoring.description: Health, deployments, costs, and activity in one place.
monitoring.name: NAME
monitoring.noRecentEvents: No recent events. Deploy an agent team to see activity here.

activity.title: Activity Log
activity.description: Track all operations — deploys, scales, destroys, and configuration changes.
activity.operationsWillAppear: Operations like deploy, destroy, and scale will appear here automatically.

settings.pageDescription: Theme, language, providers, and system preferences.
settings.configureLLMProviders: Configure LLM providers and API keys for agent deployments.
settings.configureProvidersMetadata: Configure provider metadata here. Store provider credentials in Secrets & Environment.
settings.themeSectionDescription: Choose how the console looks in this browser.
settings.languageSectionDescription: Choose which language the console uses.
settings.noProvidersConfigured: No providers configured. Add one to get started.
settings.credentialsManagedInSecrets: Sensitive credentials now live in Secrets & Environment instead of settings.json.
settings.credentialsMoveNotice: Provider API keys are now managed as encrypted environment values. Add keys like OPENAI_API_KEY on the Secrets & Environment page.
settings.aboutDescription: Shadow Cloud Console provides full lifecycle management for AI agent clusters on Kubernetes. Deploy, monitor, scale, and manage your agent teams with an intuitive cloud-native interface.
settings.communityDescription: Connect to the Shadow community hub to browse and fork templates shared by the community.
settings.communityBaseUrlHint: The URL of the Shadow community server. Defaults to https://shadowob.com.
settings.communityTokenHint: Personal access token for the community server. Leave blank to use OAuth instead.
settings.communityOAuthHint: Open the community server in a popup to authorize with your account.
settings.communityOAuthError: OAuth authorization failed. Please try again.
settings.communityBrowseHint: Browse the full template catalog on the community server.

deployTask.title: Deployment Task
deployTask.description: Track a deploy task through completion and reopen it later from the same fixed URL.
deployTask.taskNotFoundDescription: The requested deployment task does not exist or has already been removed.
deployTask.invalidTaskId: The deployment task URL is invalid.
deployTask.linkCopyFailed: Failed to copy deployment task link
deployTask.openTaskDescription: Resume live progress and revisit the full deploy log later.
deployTask.listDescription: Track active and completed deployments, then reopen any task from one place.
deployTask.noTasksDescription: Start a deployment from the store and it will appear here.
deployTask.logDescription: Historical logs are replayed first, then live output continues automatically while the task is still running.
deployTask.runningDescription: You can leave this page at any time and come back later with the same URL.
deployTask.destroySuccessDescription: Pulumi completed the destroy and verified the namespace was removed.
deployTask.blockedByDescription: Waiting for #{{id}} ({{status}}) to finish before continuing.
deployTask.cancelTaskDescription: Request cancellation for this deployment task. Queued tasks stop immediately; running Pulumi operations receive a cooperative cancel signal.
deployTask.successDescription: The full task history stays available here for later review.
deployTask.failedDescription: The failure details and full log output are preserved here for debugging.

validate.title: Validate Config
validate.description: Paste or edit a shadowob-cloud.json config and validate it.

community.title: Community Templates
community.description: Browse templates shared by the Shadow community.
community.forkError: Failed to fork template. Please try again.
community.noTemplatesHint: Try a different search or check your community server connection.
community.errorHint: Check your community server URL and token in Settings → Community.

wallet.title: Wallet & Billing
wallet.description: Your Shrimp Coin balance and transaction history.
wallet.noTransactionsDesc: Your billing history will appear here once you deploy an app.
